rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY MURRAY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 8, 2026, line 1. RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES AGENCY, TRENT RHORER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. The court needs additional time. Continued on the court's own motion to January 28, 2026. If the selected date is inconvenient to the parties, they may email the clerk with other mutually agreeable dates. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL QUINTANA VS. DUNG VU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Yaelle E. Gurel; [Proposed] Order (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 8, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANT DUNG VU'S Motion To Vacate Dismissal. The unopposed motion to vacate dismissal is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Defendant Robert Bosch Semiconductor Llcs Reply In Support Of Defendants Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 8, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Seal Defendant Robert Bosch Semiconductor LLCs Reply In Support Of Defendants Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint. The unopposed motion to seal is granted. The court finds that the information sought to be sealed concerns internal company financial information, disclosure of which is likely to cause it financial harm. Pakal has narrowly tailored its sealing request. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: TAMARA THOMAS VS. SMI IMAGING, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 8, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF TAMARA THOMAS'S Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Action. The motion is continued to January 27, 2026, and the court orders supplemental briefing. Labor Code 2699, subdivision (s)(2), provides that "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part. The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This court's view is that settlement of a civil action filed pursuant to PAGA, even one that is subsequently settled on an individual basis, requires court approval and LWDA submission. In particular, the moving party must provide information from which the court can discern that the resolution is fair to absent aggrieved employees. That they can file their own claims because their dismissal is without prejudice is not sufficient. Plaintiff may provide additional facts showing that the resolution is fair to absent aggrieved employees in a supplemental brief filed no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
